steps.label: Household Member Information Entry
steps.description: On this screen, you will enter details for each member of your household. Please provide accurate information for all individuals living at your address. Instructions are provided for each field, and you may add multiple household members. This information helps determine eligibility and ensures your application is processed correctly.

fields.label: Age
fields.error_message: Age is required and must be a valid number.

data_validation: Cannot have more than one 'Self' entry.
Age must be a positive number.
Address must be a valid format.
If 'Child' is selected, age must be less than 18.
If 'Parent' is selected, age must be at least 18.
No duplicate household members (same first and last name, age, and relationship).

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to enter household income information.

ACTION_1.name: Trigger Household Income Entry
ACTION_1.implementation: Navigates to the Household Income Entry screen upon clicking the Next button.

steps.label: Household Income Entry
steps.description: On this screen, you will enter your household's total monthly income. Please provide accurate financial information, as this will be used to determine your eligibility for SNAP benefits. Instructions are provided for each field, and you may review your entries before proceeding.

fields.label: Monthly Income
fields.error_message: Monthly Income is required and must be a valid amount.

data_validation: Monthly Income must be a positive currency value.
Total Household Income must equal the sum of all monthly incomes entered.

buttons.label: Next
buttons.description: Proceed to review and submit your application.

ACTION_2.name: Trigger Income Eligibility Check
ACTION_2.implementation: Initiates the income eligibility check process after completing the Household Income Entry screen.

steps.label: Check Income Eligibility
steps.description: System compares the entered total household income against the current SNAP eligibility threshold to determine if the applicant is eligible for SNAP benefits.

rules.label: Income Eligibility Comparison
rules.logic: If Total Household Income (or Monthly Income) is less than or equal to the current SNAP eligibility threshold, then the applicant is eligible to proceed. If Total Household Income (or Monthly Income) exceeds the threshold, the applicant is ineligible and should be notified.

ACTION_3.name: Trigger Application Review Screen
ACTION_3.implementation: Navigates to the Application Review and Submission screen if income eligibility validation passes.
ACTION_3.triggerCondition: Income is within the eligibility threshold.

ACTION_4.name: Trigger Income Ineligibility Notification
ACTION_4.implementation: Navigates to the Income Ineligibility Notification screen if income eligibility validation fails.

steps.label: Application Review and Submission
steps.description: This screen allows you to review all the information you have entered for your SNAP application, including household member details and income information. Please carefully check all entries for accuracy. You may go back to edit any section if needed. Once you are satisfied, submit your application for processing.
data_validation: All required fields must be completed before submission.
Household and income information must be reviewed and confirmed.
No missing or invalid data in displayed sections.

buttons.label: Submit Application
buttons.description: Submit your completed application for SNAP eligibility review.

buttons.label: Edit Household Information
buttons.description: Return to the household information entry screen to make changes.

buttons.label: Edit Income Information
buttons.description: Return to the income entry screen to make changes.

ACTION_5.name: Mark Workflow Completion
ACTION_5.implementation: Marks the workflow as completed after the application is reviewed and submitted.

steps.label: Submit Application
steps.description: System validates the application data entered by the applicant, saves the application record, generates an audit log entry, and prepares the record for assignment to a caseworker.

rules.label: Application Data Validation
rules.logic: Validate that all required fields in Display: Household Information and Display: Income Information are present and correctly formatted before submission.

rules.label: Save Application Record
rules.logic: Persist the validated application data to the system database.

rules.label: Generate Audit Log
rules.logic: Create an audit log entry capturing the submission event, including timestamp, applicant identifier, and summary of submitted data.

rules.label: Prepare Record for Assignment
rules.logic: Mark the application record as ready for assignment to a caseworker for further processing.

ACTION_6.name: Return to Household Information Entry
ACTION_6.implementation: Navigates back to the Household Information Entry screen for corrections.

steps.label: Income Ineligibility Notification
steps.description: This screen notifies you that your household income exceeds the current SNAP eligibility threshold. You will see a clear message explaining the reason for ineligibility and guidance on next steps, including information about other resources or how to appeal if you believe this is an error.
data_validation: Display message only if household income exceeds eligibility threshold.
Ensure message is clear and actionable.

buttons.label: Return to Application
buttons.description: Go back to review or update your application.

steps.label: Assign to Caseworker
steps.description: System assigns the submitted SNAP application record to a designated caseworker for further processing. The assignment is based on predefined caseworker allocation logic, which may include workload balancing, specialization, or geographic region. The system ensures the application record is flagged as 'Assigned' and updates the caseworker's queue. Audit logs are generated for traceability.

assignments.label: Assign to Caseworker
assignments.logic: Upon successful submission and validation of the application, the system evaluates available caseworkers based on assignment rules (e.g., workload, region, specialization) and assigns the application record to the selected caseworker. The assignment is recorded in the system, and the caseworker's queue is updated. An audit log entry is created to document the assignment event.

ACTION_7.name: Assign Application to Caseworker
ACTION_7.implementation: Assigns the application record to a designated caseworker based on predefined allocation logic.
ACTION_7.triggerCondition: Application submission is validated and marked ready for assignment.

steps.label: Caseworker Application Processing Task
steps.description: As a caseworker, you are assigned this application for further review and action. On this screen, you will see all submitted household and income information, along with any system-generated notes or audit logs. You may update the application status, request additional information, or proceed with eligibility determination. Guidance and instructions are provided for each action.
data_validation: Caseworker must review all displayed information before taking action.
Application status updates must be logged.
Any requests for additional information must be documented.

buttons.label: Mark as Reviewed
buttons.description: Confirm that you have reviewed the application.

buttons.label: Request More Information
buttons.description: Send a request to the applicant for additional details.

buttons.label: Proceed with Eligibility Determination
buttons.description: Continue with the eligibility review process.
